denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 05/21/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100934442          Group #: GRP-1118
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-120187
Claim #: CLM-2026-194255
Date of Service (requested): 05/01/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Provider of record

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 05/03/2026 for the requested service
Diagnosis (as submitted): Not itemized in this notice
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM138 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION

MCG Care Guidelines (edition not specified) were referenced without module code or edition year.

Our review applied the criteria in the applicable coverage policy and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), including utilization-management provisions. The information on file does not demonstrate that the requested service is medically necessary for this member at this time. The service is denied because plan criteria were not met. Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy utilization-management requirements for the benefit category under review. The record did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met.

Additional plan language regarding experimental/investigational definitions, network status, and member cost-sharing may apply and is available upon request.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100934442) and reference UM-2026-120187. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 19-year-old member has Pilonidal disease, recurrent (L05.01). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Excision with flap closure recommended as the next appropriate step. Three flares in 18 months; failed wound care. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for Excision with flap closure in the setting of Pilonidal disease, recurrent (L05.01); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: The brief matches the assigned UHC / surgical cell and executes sub_tactic 'duration_excessive' with a clinically appropriate Pilonidal disease, recurrent (L05.01) → Excision with flap closure pair.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic UHC correspondence with UnitedHealthcare medical policy and procedural tone.
